C++ Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check for correct syntax, variable declarations, control flow, loops, pointers, and array usage.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax, Variables & Data Types
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Profile Setup
Declare variables for a user: `username` (string), `level` (int), `score` (double), and `isActive` (bool). Print them.
lessons.hint: Use string, int, double, bool. Include <iostream> and <string>.

lessons.title: Control Flow (If/Else & Switch)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Grade Evaluator
Write an if/else block that prints 'Pass' if `score` is >= 60, otherwise 'Fail'.

lessons.title: Loops & Functions
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Multiply Function
Create a function `multiply` that takes two integers and returns their product. Call it and print the result.
lessons.hint: Return type is int. Parameters are int a, int b.

lessons.title: Pointers & References (The Basics)
lessons.theory:
## Pointers vs References
Pointers (`*`) hold memory addresses. References (`&`) are aliases for variables.
```cpp
int x = 10;
int* ptr = &x; // Pointer
int& ref = x;  // Reference
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Pointer Setup
Create an integer `val`, a pointer `p` pointing to `val`, and a reference `r` to `val`.

lessons.title: Arrays & Strings
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Array Sum
Create an array of 3 integers and calculate their sum using a loop.
lessons.hint: Loop until i < 3. Add arr[i] to sum.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of C++ Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding C++ Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of C++ Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'C++ Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'C++ Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for C++ Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering C++ Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to C++ Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in C++ Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying C++ Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure C++ Fundamentals projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'C++ Fundamentals'.

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in C++ Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in C++ Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in C++ Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling C++ Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: C++ Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'C++ Fundamentals Final'.

Object-Oriented C++.aiRubric: Check class design, constructors, access specifiers, inheritance, polymorphism, and operator overloading.

lessons.title: Classes, Objects & Access Specifiers
lessons.instructions:
## Task: User Class
Create a `User` class with a private `name` and public `setName` and `getName` methods.
lessons.hint: Use private for fields, public for methods.
lessons.rubric: Class fields and methods have correct access specifiers.

lessons.title: Constructors & Destructors
lessons.instructions:
## Task: File Handler
Create a `File` class with a constructor that prints 'Opened' and a destructor that prints 'Closed'.

lessons.title: Inheritance
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Employee Hierarchy
Create `Manager` that inherits publicly from `Employee`.

lessons.title: Polymorphism & Virtual Functions
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Shape Area
Create a base class `Shape` with a virtual `area()` method. Override it in `Circle`.
lessons.hint: Use virtual in Base, override in Derived.
lessons.rubric: Virtual function and override used correctly.

lessons.title: Operator Overloading
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Add Points
Overload the `+` operator to add two `Point` objects together.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Object-Oriented C++.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Object-Oriented C++.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Object-Oriented C++.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Modern C++ & Memory.aiRubric: Check dynamic memory, smart pointers, STL usage, templates, and exception handling.

lessons.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation (new & delete)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Dynamic Array
Allocate an array of 5 integers dynamically, then delete it.

lessons.title: Smart Pointers (unique_ptr & shared_ptr)
lessons.theory:
## Smart Pointers
Automatic memory management in modern C++.
```cpp
std::unique_ptr<int> p = std::make_unique<int>(10);
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Unique Pointer
Create a `unique_ptr` for an integer and print its value.

lessons.title: The Standard Template Library (STL & Vectors)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Vector Usage
Create a vector of integers, add '10' and '20' to it, and print the first element.

lessons.title: Templates (Generic Programming)
lessons.theory:
## Templates
Write generic functions and classes.
```cpp
template <typename T>
T getMax(T a, T b) { return (a > b) ? a : b; }
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Generic Swap
Create a template function `mySwap` that swaps two values of any type.

lessons.title: Exception Handling
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Try/Catch Block
Throw a `runtime_error` if `divisor` is 0, and catch it to print the message.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.title: Logging and Monitoring
lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Modern C++ & Memory
lessons.theory: ## Reusable Solutions\nDesign patterns solve common architectural problems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Connecting Services\nAPIs allow different systems to communicate.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Database Connectivity
lessons.theory: ## Data Access\nConnecting to a DB safely is essential.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Connection String\n1. Set `conn_str` to 'postgres://localhost'.

lessons.title: Scaling Applications
lessons.theory: ## Horizontal Scaling\nAdding more instances handles increased load.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Instance Count\n1. Set `instances` to 3.

lessons.title: Microservices Architecture
lessons.theory: ## Decoupling\nMicroservices break large apps into independent services.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Service Name\n1. Set `service` to 'Auth'.

lessons.title: Best Practices and Anti-patterns
lessons.theory: ## Writing Clean Code\nMaintainability ensures the long-term success of Modern C++ & Memory projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clean Code\n1. Set `is_clean` to True.
